# Transcrição: Communication_exists_on_a_continuum

- **Fonte:** Communication_exists_on_a_continuum
- **Duração:** 01:13
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:13]

People say, oh, all your videos are edited. Are you editing out all of your terrible communication? Are you editing out only the best bits? And I kind of wanted to do a video where I'm speaking nonstop for, you know, 30 minutes, an hour, whatever it may be. Not really to go, hey, look at me. I'm a great communicator, but really to show you that great communication exists on a continuum. There are moments where we communicate really well. And then there are moments where we're kind of clunky and all over the place and rambly. It exists on a continuum. There are times when you're great and the times when you're not. The thing that you have to become conscious of is that in the moments where you need to be great, do you have the skills to rise to the occasion? Because what tends to happen is that a lot of the times in high pressure situations where you're going for a job interview or you're having a conversation with someone that you have a crush on, in those high stake moments, do you rise to the occasion with great communication skills? Are you able to connect with the person on the other side of the table? It's not about always being great at this thing. No, there are times when I'm a terrible communicator. And that's often times when I'm just low energy, I'm tired, I'm not focused. It exists on a continuum.
